title: long-fermentation adventurous tunisian egg noodles Pasta
description: fatty, shaggy, adventurous tunisian dish featuring egg noodles, long-fermentation-dough, and paleo-friendly ingredients
instructions: Divide the long-fermentation dough into 4 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the ground lamb and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped garlic, parsley, kosher salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the egg noodles according to package instructions until al dente.
Roll out the cooked egg noodles and cut them into rough rectangular shapes.

Assemble the long-fermentation adventurous tunisian egg noodles Pasta by placing a piece of rolled-out dough on a flat surface, topping it with a portion of the cooked egg noodles, and folding the dough over to form a rectangle.
Repeat with the remaining dough and egg noodles, and serve hot.
Optional: top with crumbled feta cheese for a non-paleo version.
